findings: Candidate depicts a single, anatomically coherent Osprey: one head, one hooked black bill, two folded wings, two long legs with large curved talons, and one barred tail.

Plumage and proportions match the supplied references and live identification sources: brown upperparts, mostly white underparts, white head with broad dark eye stripe, pale blue-gray feet, and long narrow-winged raptor structure.
All visible measurements match Cornell’s published ranges: length 54–58 cm, wingspan 150–180 cm, and weight 1.4–2.0 kg.
The visible behavior, habitat, and field-mark text is supported by the checked sources. No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.
Minor wording note only: “yellow to orange” belongs to the eye-color palette rather than plumage proper; it is visually appropriate and not a material text error.

verification_sources.title: Osprey Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
